zh.lifeSummary: 當John de la Lynde I於1374年出生在英國多瑟特文特爾布恩 可倫斯同時，他的父親William de la Lynde Jr30歲，母親Edith de la Hartley25歲。他於1403年與Isolda Heryng在英國英格蘭素可易亞斯特 格林斯亞德結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1457年在他的家鄉享年83歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Lynde
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Lind
meanings: Scandinavian, English, and Dutch: topographic name for someone who lived by a lime tree, Scandinavian and Middle English lind. As a Swedish name, it is often of ornamental origin.
North German and Danish: habitational name from any of various places called with Lind ‘lime tree’.
South German, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): nickname for a gentle, mild, tolerant man, from Middle High German lint, linde, Yiddish lind ‘gentle, mild’.

nameMeanings.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When John de la Lynde I was born in 1374, in Winterbourne Clenstone, Dorset, England, his father, William de la Lynde Jr, was 30 and his mother, Edith de la Hartley, was 25. He married Isolda Heryng in 1403, in East Grinstead, Sussex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. He died in 1457, in his hometown, at the age of 83.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John de la Lynde I est né en 1374, à Winterbourne Clenstone, Dorset, Angleterre, son père, William de la Lynde Jr, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Edith de la Hartley, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Isolda Heryng en 1403, à East Grinstead, Sussex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en 1457, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 83 ans.

Er hat 1403 in East Grinstead, Sussex, England, United Kingdom Isolda Heryng geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Er starb 1457 im Alter von 83 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

ja.lifeSummary: John de la Lynde Iが1374年にイギリスドールセトウィンテルボウルン クレンストーンで生まれた時，父William de la Lynde Jrは30歳，母Edith de la Hartleyは25歳であった。1403年にイギリスイギリススセックスエアスト グリンステアドでIsolda Heryngと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1457年に故郷で83歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: John de la Lynde I(이)가 1374년 영국 도르세트 윈터보우른 클렌스톤에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William de la Lynde Jr(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Edith de la Hartley(은)는 25세였다. 그는 1403년 영국 잉글랜드 수세스 이스트 그린스테아드에서 Isolda Heryng(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1457년 그의 고향에서 83세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ele casou-se com Isolda Heryng em 1403, em East Grinstead, Sussex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1457, em sua cidade natal, com 83 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John de la Lynde I родился в 1374 г. в местности винтирбурн кленстоун, дорсет, Англия, его отцу по имени William de la Lynde Jr было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Edith de la Hartley, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Isolda Heryng в 1403 г. в местности эаст гринстид, суссекс, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Он умер в 1457 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 83 лет.
